plainEnglish: Sometimes a video's natural pauses are too short to describe everything visual. At Level AAA, you provide extended audio description: the video pauses to let the narration catch up, then resumes. This guarantees blind users get all the visual detail even in fast-paced, densely-narrated content.
whyItMatters: Blind users miss visual detail in densely-narrated videos where there aren't enough pauses for standard audio description to fit.
quickCheck: Check whether an extended-audio-description version is offered for any video whose pauses are too brief for full standard description.
commonMistakes: Cramming descriptions into pauses that are too short, so the narration gets cut off.
Not offering an extended-description version for content-dense video.
Assuming standard audio description (1.2.5) is enough when the visuals are too dense for the available pauses.

codeExamples.label: Video offering an extended audio-description track
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/demo.mp4">
  <track
    kind="descriptions"
    src="/demo.extended-desc.vtt"
    srcLang="en"
    label="Extended audio description"
  />
</video>

codeExamples.label: Video with no extended description for dense visuals
